item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "scenario_root='longhorizon-work/scenario-02'

printf 'SEARCH batch\\n'
rg -n --hidden -i -F 'batch' \""'$scenario_root"
batch_rc=$?
printf '"'exit_status=%s\\n' \""'$batch_rc"

printf '"'SEARCH timestamps\\n'
rg -n --hidden -i -e 'generated_at' -e '\\btimestamps?\\b' -e '(?<"'![0-9])[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}(?![0-9])'"' \""'$scenario_root"
timestamps_rc=$?
printf '"'exit_status=%s\\n' \""'$timestamps_rc"

printf '"'SEARCH owner\\n'
rg -n --hidden -F 'operations' \""'$scenario_root"
owner_rc=$?
printf '"'exit_status=%s\\n' \""'$owner_rc"

printf '"'SEARCH documentation_example\\n'
rg -n --hidden -F 'onboard run --config config/onboarding.json --mode streaming --max-parallel 3 --owner operations --queue new --queue retry' \""'$scenario_root/docs/onboarding.md"
example_rc=$?
printf '"'exit_status=%s\\n' \""'$example_rc"

if [ "$batch_rc" -ne 1 ] || [ "$timestamps_rc" -ne 1 ] || [ "$owner_rc" -ne 0 ] || [ "$example_rc" -ne 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi'

item.text: The timestamp search failed because ripgrep’s default regex engine does not support look-around. I’m rerunning all four searches unchanged except for enabling PCRE2 on that one regex; this fixes the tool invocation without changing the validation contract.

item.text:
```text
SEARCH batch
exit_status=1

SEARCH timestamps
exit_status=1

SEARCH owner
longhorizon-work/scenario-02/docs/onboarding.md:3:Run streaming onboarding for the operations owner with up to three jobs in parallel, processing the `new` queue before `retry`:
longhorizon-work/scenario-02/docs/onboarding.md:6:onboard run --config config/onboarding.json --mode streaming --max-parallel 3 --owner operations --queue new --queue retry
longhorizon-work/scenario-02/config/onboarding.json:5:  "owner": "operations",
exit_status=0

SEARCH documentation_example
6:onboard run --config config/onboarding.json --mode streaming --max-parallel 3 --owner operations --queue new --queue retry
exit_status=0
```

Exit `1` for `batch` and timestamps means no matches.
